channel.title: +Proto Onge on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Proto Onge on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Indian HG
item.description: Early settlement There were early hunter gatherer (=IHG) groups - currently reflected in isolated Indian tribes. Those close to the un-admixed old Indian H-G are the south Indian tribes from the Tamil country like the Irula, Malayan, Kadar, Pulliyar and Paniya. They are the deeply divergent mainland sister group of the Onge/Jarawa of the Andamans. They represent a remnant population of early out of Africa immigrants who first reached India. There they might have mixed with one or more preexisting archaic Homo (one might have been the Denisovans) but the details of that are limited.

item.title: Melanesians
item.description: Genetics Share some 5% of genome from Denisova hominin. Range Fiji, Vanuatu, the Solomon Islands, and Papua New Guinea
